analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 (suggested direction.txt): counterparty-conditioned forward mids on Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4 trades days 1–3 joined to same-timestamp price rows. Horizons K∈{5,20,100} map to +K*100 timestamp units. Aggression: trade price vs concurrent L1 bid/ask. Spread regime: per-product tertiles on pooled tape. Script: r4_phase1_counterparty_analysis.py.
used_to_decide: Phase 1 gate object appended below (round4_phase1_complete). Phase 2 not started until sim/backtest validates top edges under worse fills.

phase1_bullets.conclusion: Strongest cell: Mark 67 aggressive buys on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT show large positive self-mid change at k=5 (n=164, mean≈1.96 ticks, t≈13) stable in tight/all bins; Mark 22 aggressive sells on VEV_5300 show negative k=5 self-mid (n=160, mean≈-0.20, t≈-3.3). Mark 55 on extract is horizon-sensitive (buy k=5/20 positive mean; sell k=100 negative).

phase1_bullets.bullet: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (pair cell means + residuals)
phase1_bullets.conclusion: Pair-conditioned means at k=20 are tabulated; residual file ranks pairs after subtracting global pair mean — dominant structure remains Mark 01→Mark 22 mass on vouchers (see initial summary), residuals highlight smaller sub-pairs for Phase-2 conditioning.

Directed edge table matches repo sketch (Mark 01→Mark 22 top count). Two-hop chains within 5k timestamp units show repeated Mark 38→Mark 14→Mark 38 with mildly negative extract forward mean (n=885) vs rare high-n tails (e.g. Mark 14→Mark 38→Mark 22 n=19 only).
387 multi-print timestamps; extract forward Δmid (k=20) mean≈0.33 (n=1608) on burst rows vs ≈0.08 (n=2671) off burst — bursts align with larger short-horizon extract drift in-sample.

When Mark 22 is aggressive seller, subsequent k=20 self-mid markout for the traded symbol is worst for prints with Mark 49 as buyer (n=12, very negative mean) and mildest for Mark 01 basket flow (n=1339 near zero) — candidate avoid-quote / fade tail vs Mark 49.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Follow Mark 67 aggressive buy prints on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT with short-horizon long bias (or avoid fading) at k=5
top_5_tradeable_edges.effect: mean Δmid ≈ +1.96 price units at k=5 on same symbol

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Treat Mark 22 aggressive sells on VEV_5300 as short-term bearish for 5300 mid (k=5)
Use same-timestamp multi-symbol bursts as extract risk-on / drift indicator (forward extract k=20)
Mark 55 on extract: separate buy vs sell horizons — aggressive buys mildly positive k=5/20; aggressive sells strongly negative k=100
Two-hop motif Mark 55→Mark 01→Mark 22 predicts negative extract forward (k=20) in bulk (n=535)

no_edge_or_negative_results: Mark 01 aggressive buy specifically on VEV_5300 at k=20: zero qualifying rows in tape slice (Mark 01→Mark 22 basket hits other lines); r4_p1_mark01_buy_vev5300_by_day_k20.csv empty — cannot claim participant alpha on that exact cell.
High-|t| two-hop tails (e.g. Mark 14→Mark 38→Mark 22) have n<20 — not stable as standalone rules.
Phase 1 is mid-only markouts; tradable PnL under worse fills not established (STRATEGY caveat applies).

analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 2: orthogonal conditioning (burst ±500 ts around any Mark01→Mark22 print), Sonic joint gate on prices merged to enriched trades, lead–lag of Mark67 signed extract flow vs extract mid on full price grid, microprice/touch proxy when gate on. Script r4_phase2_analysis.py. Sim probe trader_v1 (Sonic gate + Mark67 aggressive buy on extract from market_trades) backtested worse+all.
used_to_decide: Phase 2 gate object below. Phase 1 Mark67 extract edge survives per-day (k=5) but Sonic gate does not amplify it in the Mark67 subset (n small); burst neighborhood around Mark01→Mark22 does not change Mark67 mean. Lead–lag shows modest positive corr at 1–2 price rows. v1 sim is tiny positive PnL (98) — not validation of 200k/day bar.

phase1_interaction: Phase-1 headline (Mark67 aggressive buy extract → positive short-horizon mids) is confirmed per-day on k=5 (r4_p2_mark67_buy_extract_by_day_k5.csv). Conditioning on Sonic tight gate does not increase mean k=5 on that subset vs loose (r4_p2_sonic_gate_mark67_extract_k5.csv; n=27 tight vs 137 loose — refines Phase-1 by showing gate is not the dominant amplifier for Mark67 in this slice). Mark01→Mark22 burst neighborhood does not shift Mark67 k=20 extract forward mean (r4_p2_burst_pair_mark67_extract_k20.csv).

phase2_bullets.conclusion: Mark67|extract k=20 means identical with/without ±500ts neighborhood of Mark01→Mark22 prints; burst slice n=16 too thin to claim synergy.
When joint gate is on, VEV_5200 rows are already at spread≤2 by construction; file records share_le2≈1.0 — inner-quote opportunity still limited to tape discretization.

phase2_bullets.bullet: 3) Cross-instrument lead–lag (Mark67 signed flow vs extract)
phase2_bullets.conclusion: Lag 0 corr undefined (flow and fwd share same mid row); lags 1–2 price rows (~100–200 ts) show corr ~0.12 / ~0.095 between signed Mark67 extract flow and forward extract mid change, decaying with longer lag.

Tight gate subset mean k=5 slightly lower than loose for Mark67 buys (2.0 vs 1.78 n.s. given n) — falsifies 'gate always cleans Mark67 signal' for this cell; Phase 3 still warranted for fuller interaction.
Deferred: full R3-style smile residual × counterparty grid not computed this pass (CPU); next iteration will subsample bisection IV on VEV_5000 vs seller ID.
Minimal join-bid rule yields +98 total PnL (days 1–3) worse=all; tape edge does not yet scale — execution and selection differ from mid-only Phase 1.
Not implemented; no inventory state in v1.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Lag-1–2 price rows: Mark67 signed extract flow positively correlates with subsequent extract mid drift
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.relation_to_phase1: Supports Phase-1 directionality but at different aggregation (flow grid vs print events).

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Mark67 aggressive buy extract — per-day stability of positive k=5 forward extract mid
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.effect: mean k=5 extract forward ~1.75–2.2 ticks per day
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.relation_to_phase1: Confirms Phase-1 top cell is not a single-day artifact.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Sim: gated passive long extract after Mark67 lift is weakly profitable in aggregate
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.relation_to_phase1: Downgrades economic magnitude vs mid-only t-stats; execution matters.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Sonic gate × Mark67 (negative interaction in mean k=5) — candidate for Phase-3 three-way tests
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.effect: tight mean < loose mean on small n
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.relation_to_phase1: Refines / partially falsifies naive stacking of Sonic gate on Mark67 prints.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Burst neighborhood around Mark01→Mark22 does not improve Mark67 extract forward — avoid over-conditioning
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.effect: near-burst vs not identical mean k=20
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.relation_to_phase1: Orthogonal negative on basket-burst narrative for Mark67 overlay.
